# Smith v. Jones [2021] UKSC 45

## Facts
The plaintiff, Mr. Smith, entered into a service agreement with the defendant, Mr. Jones, on 1 January 2018. The agreement contained a termination clause requiring 90 days' notice.

## Holding
The Supreme Court held that the termination clause in the service agreement between Smith and Jones was enforceable. The court determined that 90 days constitutes reasonable notice under Section 5 of the Employment Rights Act.

## Reasoning
The court considered the precedent set in Brown v. Green [2019] and found that termination clauses providing notice periods between 30 and 180 days are presumptively reasonable.
